symptoms.symptom_name: Nocturnal retrosternal burning after a large late fatty meal

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Meal-provoked, recumbent nocturnal burning is a hallmark reflux pattern.
Acute severe retrosternal pain radiating to the neck must have a cardiac cause excluded regardless of the reflux flavour.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acid regurgitation with marked hypersalivation (water brash)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Regurgitation of acidic fluid and water brash are relatively specific for reflux.
Prominent acid regurgitation favours an oesophageal source but does not exclude coincident cardiac pathology.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symptoms worse lying flat, relieved by sitting/standing upright

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Postural (recumbent) provocation reflects loss of the gravitational anti-reflux barrier.

symptoms.symptom_name: Partial, temporary relief with a single dose of liquid antacid

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Antacid relief is consistent with acid-related pain but is not diagnostic.
Symptomatic response to an antacid does NOT reliably exclude a cardiac cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of diaphoresis, pallor, arm/jaw radiation, dyspnoea and exertional trigger

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent classic ischaemic features lower cardiac probability but cannot exclude ACS without objective testing.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic nocturnal dry cough, morning hoarseness and globus sensation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Laryngopharyngeal reflux features indicate chronic acid exposure and support a longstanding reflux diagnosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal cardiovascular examination (regular rate/rhythm, normal S1/S2, no murmurs) and clear chest on auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal cardiac exam does not exclude ACS; sealed clinician finding, unavailable to the telehealth AI Doctor.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild diffuse epigastric tenderness to deep palpation without guarding or rebound

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-specific epigastric tenderness fits acid-peptic disease; requires palpation the AI Doctor cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Blood pressure 140/85 mmHg, heart rate 92 bpm, SpO2 99%, afebrile (clinician-measured)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stable haemodynamics are reassuring but non-diagnostic; measured in person, not obtainable over chat without a validated device.

symptoms.symptom_name: 12-lead ECG and serum troponin to exclude acute coronary syndrome

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: ECG and troponin are the objective tools that exclude/confirm ACS; never available in real time over telehealth.
Confirms pathological acid exposure in refractory outpatient cases; not acutely indicated and not a telehealth investigation.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these strongly characterise meal-provoked, recumbent nocturnal reflux with laryngopharyngeal involvement.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line objective test to screen for acute coronary syndrome in any acute chest pain that cannot be excluded clinically.
Cardiac biomarker to exclude myocardial injury; requires in-person/ED pathology, not available via telehealth.
Consider at GP follow-up to guide eradication if reflux/dyspepsia persists.
Not acutely indicated; reserved for refractory outpatient cases and must not be pursued in the acute setting.
